## Plan: Custom Theme System

- `packages/shared` uses **explicit subpath exports** (no barrel), pure TypeScript modules, no barrel index

- The `SidebarFooter` already has a structure that accommodates a second menu item

### 6 changes, in order

| # | File | What changes |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **New file** — `ThemeColors`, `Theme` interfaces; 3 built-in themes; `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| 2 | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./themes"` subpath export (same pattern as `./model`, `./git`, etc.) |
| 3 | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Full replacement** — returns `{ theme: Theme, setTheme(id), availableThemes, resolvedTheme }`. Applies 9 CSS custom properties inline on `document.documentElement`, toggles `dark` class from `colorScheme`. Preserves transition-suppression pattern. Old localStorage values ("light"/"dark"/"system") silently fall back to `"t3-dark"` |
| 4 | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **New file** — `Menu`-based dropdown with `ThemeSwatches` (3 color circles per theme). Uses `PaletteIcon` from lucide |
| 5 | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant; swap in `availableThemes` from the new hook; update selection check to `theme.id === t.id`; add swatches |

### Key design decisions

## Plan: Custom Theme System

### What I found
- `packages/shared` uses **explicit subpath exports** (no barrel), pure TypeScript modules, no barrel index
- `apps/web` already has a `useTheme` hook but it only handles `"light" | "dark" | "system"` — not named themes with full color definitions
- The hook uses `useSyncExternalStore` with a module-level listener list (a clean pattern to reuse)

- The `SidebarFooter` already has a structure that accommodates a second menu item
- Existing UI primitives (`Menu`, `MenuTrigger`, `MenuPopup`, `MenuCheckboxItem`, `Button`) cover the theme selector needs without new deps

### 6 changes, in order

| # | File | What changes |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` | **New file** — `ThemeColors`, `Theme` interfaces; 3 built-in themes; `getTheme()`, `listThemes()` |
| 2 | `packages/shared/package.json` | Add `"./themes"` subpath export (same pattern as `./model`, `./git`, etc.) |
| 3 | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **Full replacement** — returns `{ theme: Theme, setTheme(id), availableThemes, resolvedTheme }`. Applies 9 CSS custom properties inline on `document.documentElement`, toggles `dark` class from `colorScheme`. Preserves transition-suppression pattern. Old localStorage values ("light"/"dark"/"system") silently fall back to `"t3-dark"` |
| 4 | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **New file** — `Menu`-based dropdown with `ThemeSwatches` (3 color circles per theme). Uses `PaletteIcon` from lucide |
| 5 | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | Remove `THEME_OPTIONS` constant; swap in `availableThemes` from the new hook; update selection check to `theme.id === t.id`; add swatches |
| 6 | `apps/web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` | Import `ThemeSelector`; add it as first `SidebarMenuItem` in `SidebarFooter` |

### Key design decisions
- **`resolvedTheme`** is preserved in the new hook (derived directly from `theme.colorScheme`) so the 4 existing consumers need zero changes
- CSS variables are applied via **`element.style.setProperty()`** (inline styles), which beats any stylesheet specificity — clean override of Tailwind's `@theme` values
- The `dark` class is still toggled on `<html>` so all `dark:` Tailwind utilities continue to work

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

## What was implemented

### 1. `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` *(new)*
Pure TypeScript module — no Effect, no external deps. Defines:
- **`ThemeColors`** interface (7 named color fields as CSS value strings)
- **`Theme`** interface (`id`, `name`, `colorScheme`, `colors`, `fontFamily`, `fontSize`)
- **3 built-in themes**: `t3-dark` (default), `t3-light`, `t3-midnight`
- **`listThemes()`** and **`getTheme(id)`** exported functions
- **`DEFAULT_THEME_ID = "t3-dark"`**

### 2. `packages/shared/package.json` *(modified)*
Added `"./themes"` to the subpath exports map, following the identical pattern of the existing `./model`, `./git`, etc. entries.

### 3. `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` *(replaced)*
Rewrote using the same `useSyncExternalStore` + module-level listener pattern. Key changes:
- `theme` is now a full `Theme` object (was `"light" | "dark" | "system"`)
- `setTheme` takes a theme ID string
- Added `availableThemes` to the return
- Applies **9 CSS custom properties** inline on `document.documentElement` (which beats any stylesheet specificity), including `--background`, `--foreground`, `--accent`, plus new surface tokens `--sidebar-bg`, `--editor-bg`, `--terminal-bg`, `--status-bar-bg`
- Still toggles the `dark` class from `theme.colorScheme`, keeping all Tailwind `dark:` utilities working
- **`resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` is preserved** — `DiffPanel`, `ChatMarkdown`, `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `ChatView` needed zero changes

### 4. `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
Compact dropdown for the sidebar footer using existing `Menu` / `MenuItem` / `MenuPopup` / `MenuTrigger` + `Button` primitives. Shows the current theme name with a `PaletteIcon`, and a popup listing each theme with **3 colour swatches** (background / accent / sidebar circles) plus a checkmark on the active theme.

### 5. `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(modified)*
- Removed the old `THEME_OPTIONS` constant (`"system"/"light"/"dark"` strings)
- Swapped `resolvedTheme` → `availableThemes` in the `useTheme()` destructure
- Appearance section now maps over `availableThemes`, checks `theme.id === t.id`, and shows colour swatches + `t.colorScheme` description per option
- Footer line now shows `theme.name` instead of the raw `resolvedTheme` string

### 6. `apps/web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` *(modified)*
Added `ThemeSelector` as the first `SidebarMenuItem` in the `SidebarFooter`, sitting above the existing Settings button.
